purpose: prevent rejected research diagnostics from contaminating current 1X2 method selection

governance.promotion_rule: only methods with stable chronological/cross-season gain may remain active research candidates

fast100_stage1_protocol.purpose: rapidly reject weak ideas and expose failure modes before expensive validation

current_research_leader.note: retrospective reference prices lack original quote timestamps; research evidence only

methods.reason: development-selected parameter changes did not improve chronological holdout; global holdout uplift -0.083pp
latest chronological test underperformed market-only by 0.333pp
only +0.067pp overall and unstable across disjoint 100-match blocks (7 wins, 8 losses, 29 ties)
market plus Elo/form/rest features underperformed market-only by 1.776pp on latest chronological test
competition filtering reduced coverage and did not beat the simpler global probability threshold on untouched test
opening-to-closing direction/movement filters did not outperform closing market probability threshold
small single-season gain failed cross-season replication; newer-season accuracy 51.487% versus market 51.761%
newer-season 2592-match test: market 53.665% versus AH-only/combined 53.125%; selective accuracy also lower
validation gain failed untouched season; selected draw override 53.125% versus market 53.665% on 2592 matches
only 3 additional hits on 5110 newer-season matches: 51.761% to 51.820% (+0.059pp); selective thresholds not consistently better
monotone odds transformations preserve within-match 1X2 ranking, so they cannot raise full-coverage Top-1 accuracy by themselves
older validation selected Average, but newer 5110-match test produced exactly the same 2645 hits (51.761%) as preferred market; competition routing was worse
older validation improved 56.303% to 58.403%, but newer 2370-match test fell from market 53.376% to 52.785% (-0.591pp)

2025/26 five-league test on 1567 matches improved opening-market Top-1 only 834 to 838 (+0.255pp); paired transitions 22 lineup-only vs 18 market-only; 16 chronological blocks split 7 wins, 2 ties, 7 losses; not stable

2025/26 1567-match test: opening market 53.223%; expected-XI valuation direct model 51.308%; conservative override produced only one extra hit (835 vs 834), and oracle actual-XI valuation upper-bound did not improve market
untouched final-100 test: market, market+manager, market+referee and combined all finished 46/100; each added model corrected one market miss while breaking one market hit
latest-100 test: fixed p>=0.58 reached 18/28 (64.286%); validation-selected margin, entropy, provider-agreement and low-dispersion gates did not improve it, ranging 58.1%-61.9% on test

safe-midtable favourites were a weak raw subgroup, but excluding them from p>=0.58 improved 2024/25 season-end 100 from 71.43% to 73.53% and reduced 2025/26 from 64.29% to 62.96%; no stable incremental gate
season-end p>=0.65 favourites collapsed to 55.56% in 2025/26 but were 86.36% in 2024/25; home p>=0.65 was 50% versus 85%; the apparent overconfidence cap reversed across seasons
latest 2025/26 final-100 market accuracy was 46%; Bundesliga 30%, EPL/FRA 36.36%, LaLiga 54.84%, Serie A 53.85%; used only to localize errors and not as a prediction rule

use Fast100 first on structurally new prematch information; ordinary market transforms, AH/OU, form/rest, shots, predicted XI, player valuation, manager/referee and simple late-season gates are now empirically exhausted or unstable
